title: doctor codex resolves hook handlers at host topology (.bee/bin/hooks), not hard-coded root hooks/ (review P1-1)
action: In skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs doctor section (advisor conditions folded): adopt the DUAL-LOCATION existence check the Claude-side resolver already uses (bee.mjs:2664-2665) as the primary mechanism — for each handler filename from doctorHookHandlerFilenames, resolvable = exists at .bee/bin/hooks/<f> OR hooks/<f>; report WHICH root resolved in the row evidence. Port the one-line repoOwnsHookCatalog helper (fs.existsSync(root/hooks/catalog.mjs), from onboard_bee.mjs:2062) into bee.mjs for evidence labeling — it does NOT exist in bee.mjs today, a bare reference would ReferenceError (advisor R1). Do NOT attempt shell expansion of the "$r" command strings; if you additionally parse command prefixes, any unparseable command must degrade to the dual-check (advisor R2). Fixtures (advisor R3): the new NORMAL-host-topology conformance fixture must render its .codex/hooks.json COMMAND strings with the host prefix ("$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/<f>) AND plant files under .bee/bin/hooks/ with root hooks/ absent -> hook_handlers_resolvable ok; missing handler -> not ok; keep scenario 12 (bee-repo topology, root hooks/) untouched and green. Sync all bee.mjs byte mirrors (.bee/bin, .claude, .agents) and regenerate manifest.

truths: a normal host repo (.bee/bin/hooks only, no root hooks/) gets hook_handlers_resolvable ok from doctor --runtime codex
a missing handler file is detected as not-ok on host topology
bee's own repo topology (root hooks/) still resolves green (dogfood unchanged)
handler paths are parsed from the real .codex/hooks.json commands, not assumed from a directory name
all bee.mjs byte mirrors identical; manifest check green

verify: node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Doctor codex now resolves handlers at host topology via dual-location check with topology-labeled evidence; host-topology conformance fixture added; dogfood topology unchanged; independent verify green on main 2bfa034
trace.verification_evidence:
# p162-1 — done report (doctor host-topology hook resolution)

Worker: p162-1-worker (sonnet, isolated harness worktree). Worker commit
`05b4bd1`, merged into main as `2bfa034`. 2026-07-19.

## What shipped
- `doctorHookHandlersResolvable(root)` (templates/bee.mjs:2557) drops the
  hard-coded `hooksDir` param: per handler filename it checks
  `.bee/bin/hooks/<f>` OR `hooks/<f>` (dual-location, mirroring the
  Claude-side resolver precedent); evidence names which location resolved or
  what is missing under both. Call site (bee.mjs:2792) updated.
- `repoOwnsHookCatalog(root)` ported into bee.mjs:2545 (advisor R1 —
  previously existed only in onboard_bee.mjs) for topology labeling.
- No shell expansion of `"$r"` strings (advisor R2 honored).
- Conformance scenario 13: NORMAL host topology (commands
  `"$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/<f>`, files only under `.bee/bin/hooks/`, root
  `hooks/` absent) → `hook_handlers_resolvable` ok; missing-handler negative
  warns naming the file. Scenario 12 (bee-repo topology) untouched, green.
- Deviation (accepted): test_bee_cli's missing-handler fixture was silently
  passing under dual-location because it unconditionally wrote two
  overlapping stubs into `.bee/bin/hooks/` — fixed to respect
  `withHandlerFiles`.
- 5 byte mirrors + rendered trees + manifest regenerated.

## Verification (fresh command output)
Independent orchestrator re-run on main after merge 2bfa034:

```
node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs \
&& node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check
-> P1621_VERIFY_GREEN (conformance ALL PASS 8/8 incl. scenario 13; bee_cli 180/0;
   mirror 19+10 byte-identical; manifest 354 match)
```
